description: Producer DID resolution that targets the cloud-metadata endpoint (169.254.169.254 — AWS / GCP / Azure IMDS) MUST be refused. A registry that resolves a producer did:web without IP-range filtering can be coerced into reading IAM credentials, instance identity documents, and other secrets from the metadata service. See RFC-ACDP-0008 §4.8.
ssrf_rule: RFC-ACDP-0008 §4.8 (Producer DID resolution SSRF protection — link-local / IMDS)

input.scenario: A publish request whose body.agent_id is a did:web identifier resolving to the IMDS address
input.additional_test_cases_note: metadata.example.com is a hostname that itself resolves (via DNS) to 169.254.169.254 — the filter MUST run on the post-DNS IP, not the literal authority string. 169.254.1.1 is a non-IMDS link-local address and MUST also be refused (169.254.0.0/16, fe80::/10).

expected.behavior: The resolver MUST refuse before connecting. The full link-local range (169.254.0.0/16, fe80::/10) MUST be filtered, with 169.254.169.254 explicitly rejected. Filtering MUST occur on the resolved IP, so a hostname that resolves to a link-local address is caught even though its authority string looks innocuous.
expected.rationale: The cloud-metadata endpoint exposes IAM role credentials and instance identity. An unfiltered DID resolver is a direct path to full cloud-account compromise.
